description: Project has valid package.json with all required dependencies
steps: Step 1: Verify package.json exists
Step 2: Check that dependencies include youtubei.js and a Gemini SDK package
Step 3: Check that devDependencies include wrangler, esbuild, typescript, @cloudflare/workers-types
Step 4: Verify type is set to module

description: TypeScript config is valid and strict mode is enabled
steps: Step 1: Verify tsconfig.json exists
Step 2: Check module is ESNext and moduleResolution is bundler
Step 3: Check strict is true
Step 4: Check types include @cloudflare/workers-types

description: Wrangler config has nodejs_compat flag and KV binding
steps: Step 1: Verify wrangler.toml exists
Step 2: Check compatibility_flags includes nodejs_compat
Step 3: Check main points to src/backend/index.ts
Step 4: Check KV namespace binding is defined

description: Build script bundles frontend with esbuild
steps: Step 1: Verify build.mjs exists
Step 2: Check entryPoints targets src/frontend/app.js
Step 3: Check outdir is public
Step 4: Check bundle is true and format is esm

description: Smoke test passes: build, dev server, static assets, chapter detection, default-mode generation, 5W1H summary, and error handling all work end-to-end
steps: Step 1: Run node build.mjs and start wrangler dev --port 34997
Step 2: curl http://localhost:34997/ returns HTTP 200 with HTML content
Step 3: curl http://localhost:34997/app.js returns HTTP 200 with JavaScript content
Step 4: POST to /api/generate with demo video URL and default mode returns SSE stream with session ID header
Step 5: POST to /api/5w1h with valid session ID and chapter title returns all six 5W1H fields
Step 6: POST to /api/generate with invalid URL returns 4xx error
Step 7: POST to /api/5w1h with empty body returns 4xx error

description: Dev server starts without errors and serves the app
steps: Step 1: Run npm install
Step 2: Run node build.mjs
Step 3: Run npx wrangler dev --port 34997
Step 4: curl http://localhost:34997/ returns HTTP 200
Step 5: curl http://localhost:34997/app.js returns HTTP 200
Step 6: curl http://localhost:34997/app.css returns HTTP 200

description: YouTube URL parsing extracts video ID from youtube.com format
steps: Step 1: Call extractVideoId with https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xRh2sVcNXQ8
Step 2: Verify returned video ID is xRh2sVcNXQ8

description: YouTube URL parsing extracts video ID from youtu.be format
steps: Step 1: Call extractVideoId with https://youtu.be/xRh2sVcNXQ8
Step 2: Verify returned video ID is xRh2sVcNXQ8

description: Subtitle extraction returns clean text without timestamps
steps: Step 1: Fetch subtitles for video xRh2sVcNXQ8
Step 2: Verify returned text contains no timestamp patterns like 00:00 or -->
Step 3: Verify returned text is non-empty plain text

description: Subtitle language fallback chain works: zh-CN preferred over en
steps: Step 1: Fetch subtitles with language preference zh-CN
Step 2: If zh-CN available, verify Chinese content returned
Step 3: If zh-CN unavailable, verify system falls back to en

description: Webshare TCP Socket proxy mode activates on captcha detection
steps: Step 1: Simulate direct youtubei.js fetch failure due to captcha
Step 2: Verify the system retries via Webshare TCP Socket proxy
Step 3: Verify subtitles are still extracted successfully

description: Hard-coded subtitle fallback loads when direct and proxy both fail
steps: Step 1: Make both direct and proxy subtitle fetch fail
Step 2: Verify system loads subtitles from src/fallback-subtitles/demo.txt
Step 3: Verify fallback content is non-empty and usable

description: Subtitle fallback chain never breaks — each step degrades gracefully
steps: Step 1: Test direct fetch → proxy → hard-coded chain end-to-end
Step 2: Verify each fallback step logs appropriately
Step 3: Verify the app never crashes or hangs on subtitle failure

description: Non-subtitle videos show clear error message, not a crash
steps: Step 1: Request generation for a YouTube video with no subtitles
Step 2: Verify a user-friendly error message is returned
Step 3: Verify the server does not crash

description: Streaming article generation produces SSE chunks via AI provider (Gemini generateContentStream or DeepSeek chat completions with stream:true)
steps: Step 1: Call AI provider streaming endpoint with subtitle text
Step 2: Verify chunks are streamed back progressively
Step 3: Verify each chunk is well-formed SSE data

description: Generated article is in Chinese and contains Markdown headings
steps: Step 1: Stream article generation for demo video subtitles
Step 2: Verify the output is in Chinese
Step 3: Verify the output contains ## chapter headings for structure

description: Custom generation rules (taskType, style, audience, constraint) influence output
steps: Step 1: Generate article with rule containing audience: beginners
Step 2: Generate article without any rule
Step 3: Verify the rule-influenced output differs in tone or content

description: 5W1H summary returns valid JSON with all six keys via active AI provider
steps: Step 1: Call AI provider (Gemini generateContent or DeepSeek chat completions) for 5W1H with chapter context and full article
Step 2: Verify response is valid JSON
Step 3: Verify JSON contains who, what, when, where, why, how keys

description: 5W1H summary is context-aware — references full article, not just chapter
steps: Step 1: Generate 5W1H via active AI provider for a chapter that depends on earlier context
Step 2: Verify the summary reflects information from other parts of the article

description: AI provider API failures return clear error messages, not raw stack traces
steps: Step 1: Simulate AI provider API failure (invalid key or network error)
Step 2: Verify a user-friendly error message is returned
Step 3: Verify no raw stack traces or API keys are exposed

description: AI provider selection via ?provider= query parameter — gemini is default, deepseek on ?provider=deepseek
steps: Step 1: POST to /api/generate without ?provider — verify Gemini is used
Step 2: POST to /api/generate?provider=deepseek — verify DeepSeek is used
Step 3: POST to /api/generate?provider=unknown — verify falls back to Gemini

description: DeepSeek streaming article generation via OpenAI-compatible chat completions API (stream: true)
steps: Step 1: Configure DEEPSEEK_API_KEY and start dev server
Step 2: POST to /api/generate with valid youtubeUrl
Step 3: Verify SSE chunks arrive via DeepSeek (choices[0].delta.content format)
Step 4: Verify stream completes with [DONE] signal

description: DeepSeek 5W1H generation returns valid structured JSON via non-streaming chat completions
steps: Step 1: Complete an article generation via DeepSeek and note sessionId
Step 2: POST to /api/5w1h with sessionId and chapter title
Step 3: Verify response is valid JSON with who/what/when/where/why/how keys
Step 4: Verify content is context-aware Chinese text

description: Dual-format prompt templates: plain text for Gemini (buildArticlePrompt/build5W1HPrompt) and chat messages for DeepSeek (buildArticleMessages/build5W1HMessages)
steps: Step 1: Verify prompts.ts exports both plain-text and chat-message builders
Step 2: Verify Gemini path uses plain-text prompts
Step 3: Verify DeepSeek path uses chat messages with system+user roles
Step 4: Verify both formats produce equivalent generation quality

description: DeepSeek API errors are caught and returned as user-friendly SSE error messages
steps: Step 1: Configure an invalid DEEPSEEK_API_KEY
Step 2: POST to /api/generate with valid youtubeUrl
Step 3: Verify SSE stream returns data: ERROR: with clear message, not raw stack trace

description: DeepSeek uses deepseek-v4-flash model with 1M context window and no max_tokens limit
steps: Step 1: Check deepseek.ts MODEL is deepseek-v4-flash
Step 2: Verify no max_tokens in streaming request body
Step 3: Generate article and verify output exceeds 4096 tokens when needed

description: SSE output uses sentence-level batching — accumulates tokens to 。！？\n or 40-char threshold before flushing
steps: Step 1: Generate article via DeepSeek
Step 2: Verify SSE messages are sentences/phrases, not individual characters
Step 3: Verify each chunk ends at sentence boundary or exceeds threshold

description: KV session is saved on article completion with correct schema
steps: Step 1: Complete an article generation stream
Step 2: Read KV at key session:{uuid}
Step 3: Verify value contains fullText, chapters (array), and subtitle fields

description: KV session can be retrieved for 5W1H generation
steps: Step 1: Save a session to KV
Step 2: Read the session back using the session ID
Step 3: Verify all fields match what was saved

description: KV session TTL is set to 3600 seconds
steps: Step 1: Save a session to KV
Step 2: Verify the expiration/ttl is set to 3600 seconds

description: Expired or missing session returns appropriate error for 5W1H
steps: Step 1: Request 5W1H with a non-existent sessionId
Step 2: Verify a clear error response is returned (not a crash)
Step 3: Verify HTTP status is 4xx

description: After generation, 5W1H queries work across page refreshes via KV persistence
steps: Step 1: Generate an article and note the session ID
Step 2: Simulate a page refresh (new request with same sessionId)
Step 3: Verify 5W1H queries still work using the persisted KV session

description: GET / serves the HTML frontend page
steps: Step 1: curl http://localhost:34997/
Step 2: Verify HTTP status is 200
Step 3: Verify Content-Type is text/html

description: GET /public/* serves bundled static assets
steps: Step 1: curl http://localhost:34997/app.js
Step 2: Verify HTTP status is 200 and Content-Type is application/javascript
Step 3: curl http://localhost:34997/app.css
Step 4: Verify HTTP status is 200 and Content-Type is text/css

description: POST /api/generate returns SSE stream with X-Session-Id header
steps: Step 1: POST to /api/generate with valid youtubeUrl
Step 2: Verify Content-Type is text/event-stream
Step 3: Verify X-Session-Id header is present in the response
Step 4: Verify SSE data chunks are streamed

description: POST /api/generate validates youtubeUrl format and returns 4xx on invalid input
steps: Step 1: POST to /api/generate with youtubeUrl: not-a-url
Step 2: Verify HTTP status is 4xx
Step 3: Verify response body contains a descriptive error message

description: POST /api/generate returns 4xx when youtubeUrl is missing from body
steps: Step 1: POST to /api/generate with empty JSON body {}
Step 2: Verify HTTP status is 4xx
Step 3: Verify error message indicates missing youtubeUrl

description: POST /api/5w1h returns JSON with correct structure
steps: Step 1: POST to /api/5w1h with valid sessionId and chapter
Step 2: Verify HTTP status is 200
Step 3: Verify Content-Type is application/json
Step 4: Verify response has who, what, when, where, why, how fields

description: POST /api/5w1h returns 4xx when sessionId or chapter is missing
steps: Step 1: POST to /api/5w1h with empty body {}
Step 2: Verify HTTP status is 4xx
Step 3: Verify error message indicates missing parameters

description: CORS headers are present on all API responses
steps: Step 1: Send OPTIONS request to /api/generate
Step 2: Verify Access-Control-Allow-Origin header is present
Step 3: Verify Access-Control-Allow-Methods includes POST
Step 4: Repeat for /api/5w1h

description: Input area renders with YouTube URL field, custom rules textarea, model selector, and Generate button (single-click flow)
steps: Step 1: Open http://localhost:34997 in browser
Step 2: Verify YouTube URL input field is visible in the input area
Step 3: Verify custom rules textarea is visible in the input area
Step 4: Verify AI model selector dropdown is visible
Step 5: Verify Generate button (生成文章) is visible
Step 6: Verify no Fetch Chapters or chapter selection elements are visible

description: YouTube URL input shows inline validation error for invalid format
steps: Step 1: Open the page and type an invalid URL in the YouTube field
Step 2: Click Generate or blur the field
Step 3: Verify an inline error message appears immediately

description: Generate button is disabled during active generation and re-enables after completion
steps: Step 1: Enter a valid YouTube URL and click Generate
Step 2: Verify the Generate button becomes disabled immediately
Step 3: Verify the button shows '生成中...' text during generation
Step 4: Verify the button re-enables and shows '开始生成' after completion

description: SSE client connects and renders streamed text in real-time
steps: Step 1: Enter a valid YouTube URL and click Generate
Step 2: Verify text appears in the result area progressively
Step 3: Verify new chunks append to the existing content (not replace)

description: Typewriter effect renders SSE chunks as they arrive
steps: Step 1: Start article generation
Step 2: Observe the result area during streaming
Step 3: Verify content updates happen in real-time as chunks arrive
Step 4: Verify there is no long blank period before content appears

description: Markdown headings (# title, ## chapters) are rendered as HTML
steps: Step 1: Generate an article
Step 2: Verify # heading is rendered as the article title
Step 3: Verify ## headings are rendered as chapter headings
Step 4: Verify paragraphs are properly formatted

description: Chapter detection parses ## headings and injects 5W1H buttons after article completion
steps: Step 1: Generate an article with multiple chapters
Step 2: Verify each ## heading has a [5W1H] button next to it after streaming completes
Step 3: Verify buttons are injected after [DONE], not during streaming

description: Clicking [5W1H] button sends POST to /api/5w1h and renders summary
steps: Step 1: Generate an article
Step 2: Click [5W1H] button next to a chapter
Step 3: Verify a loading state is shown on the button
Step 4: Verify summary box appears with structured Who/What/When/Where/Why/How

description: 5W1H summary boxes are collapsed by default and expand on click
steps: Step 1: Generate an article and open a 5W1H summary
Step 2: Verify summary box opens
Step 3: Click again to close the summary box
Step 4: Verify the summary box collapses

description: Loading state is shown during article generation with spinner and blinking cursor
steps: Step 1: Enter a valid YouTube URL and click Generate
Step 2: Verify loading spinner appears during article generation
Step 3: Verify blinking cursor appears during SSE streaming
Step 4: Verify loading indicator and cursor disappear when streaming completes

description: Error state is rendered clearly when URL is invalid or generation fails
steps: Step 1: Enter an invalid YouTube URL and click Generate
Step 2: Verify an error message is displayed in a visible area
Step 3: Verify the error message is user-friendly and in Chinese
Step 4: Verify the input remains functional for a retry

description: 5W1H client does NOT re-transmit full article — only sends sessionId + chapter
steps: Step 1: Generate an article
Step 2: Click [5W1H] on a chapter
Step 3: Inspect the network request payload
Step 4: Verify payload contains only sessionId and chapter title, not the full article text

description: New paragraphs and chapters animate in with fadeInUp (opacity + translateY, 0.4s ease-out)
steps: Step 1: Generate an article
Step 2: Verify .animate-in class is on h1, .chapter-block, and p elements
Step 3: Verify CSS @keyframes fadeInUp exists with correct properties

description: Blinking cursor element appears during SSE streaming and is removed on completion
steps: Step 1: Start article generation
Step 2: Verify #stream-cursor element with .cursor-blink class exists during streaming
Step 3: Wait for completion and verify cursor element is removed from DOM

description: Design tokens are applied: primary #2563eb, background #ffffff
steps: Step 1: Open the page in browser
Step 2: Verify the primary button/emphasis color is #2563eb
Step 3: Verify the page background is #ffffff
Step 4: Verify body text color is #111827

description: Typography uses system-ui at 16px base with 1.8 line-height
steps: Step 1: Open the page in browser
Step 2: Inspect the body font-family — verify it is system-ui, sans-serif
Step 3: Inspect base font-size — verify 16px
Step 4: Inspect line-height — verify 1.8

description: Input elements have rounded borders, light gray background, and focus highlight
steps: Step 1: Inspect the YouTube URL input field
Step 2: Verify border-radius is applied
Step 3: Verify background is light gray
Step 4: Focus the input and verify a visible focus ring appears

description: Buttons have hover state and clear disabled state
steps: Step 1: Hover over the Generate button
Step 2: Verify the hover state is visually distinct
Step 3: Click Generate and verify the disabled state is clear (grayed out, not-allowed cursor)

description: Chapter headings have comfortable top margin to separate sections, no border boxes
steps: Step 1: Generate an article
Step 2: Verify each chapter heading (h2) has a comfortable top margin (28px) for visual separation
Step 3: Verify no .chapter-block border or padding styling is applied
Step 4: Verify chapters use natural line breaks rather than bordered cards

description: 5W1H summary box has light gray background distinct from chapter content
steps: Step 1: Open a 5W1H summary box
Step 2: Verify the summary box background differs from the article background
Step 3: Verify the summary has clear visual separation from surrounding content

description: Smooth transitions on summary box expand/collapse
steps: Step 1: Click a [5W1H] button to expand a summary
Step 2: Verify the expand animation is smooth (CSS transition)
Step 3: Click again to collapse — verify smooth collapse animation

description: Responsive layout adapts to mobile and desktop viewports
steps: Step 1: Open the page at 375px width (mobile)
Step 2: Verify input form and content are readable and not overflowing
Step 3: Resize to 1024px width (desktop)
Step 4: Verify content uses appropriate width without excessive stretching
Step 5: Verify buttons and text remain usable at all viewport sizes

description: Full flow with demo video: input URL, click Generate, streaming output, chapter rendering with 5W1H buttons
steps: Step 1: Open the page and enter https://youtu.be/xRh2sVcNXQ8
Step 2: Click Generate
Step 3: Verify loading spinner appears, then article streams in real-time
Step 4: Verify chapter headings (h2) are rendered with natural line breaks
Step 5: Verify 5W1H buttons appear next to each chapter after completion

description: End-to-end 5W1H summary flow without client re-transmission
steps: Step 1: Generate an article
Step 2: Click [5W1H] on a chapter
Step 3: Verify structured Who/What/When/Where/Why/How is displayed
Step 4: Verify network request only sends sessionId + chapter (not full article)

description: Custom generation rules observably influence AI article output
steps: Step 1: Generate with rule: audience=children, style=simple
Step 2: Generate again without any rule for the same video
Step 3: Compare outputs — verify rule-influenced version is simpler in language

description: Page refresh can still query 5W1H summaries via KV persistence
steps: Step 1: Generate an article and note a chapter title
Step 2: Refresh the page
Step 3: The article may be gone from the DOM, but verify the sessionId was saved
Step 4: Manually call POST /api/5w1h with the saved sessionId and chapter
Step 5: Verify valid 5W1H JSON is returned

description: Subtitle fallback chain works end-to-end with demo video
steps: Step 1: Block direct youtubei.js access (simulate captcha)
Step 2: Verify the system tries the Webshare proxy
Step 3: If proxy also fails, verify hard-coded fallback subtitles are used
Step 4: Verify the article is still generated successfully from fallback content

description: Invalid YouTube URL shows inline validation error without crashing the app
steps: Step 1: Enter an obviously invalid URL (not-a-youtube-link) and click Generate
Step 2: Verify a clear error message is shown in the UI
Step 3: Verify the Generate button re-enables after the error
Step 4: Verify the app is still functional for a subsequent valid request

description: Generate button re-enables after stream completion or error
steps: Step 1: Generate an article and wait for completion
Step 2: Verify the Generate button is re-enabled
Step 3: Verify a new generation can be started immediately

description: POST /api/chapters returns detected chapter list with titles and summaries from subtitle text
steps: Step 1: POST to /api/chapters with valid youtubeUrl
Step 2: Verify HTTP status is 200 and Content-Type is application/json
Step 3: Verify response contains videoTitle (string) and chapters (array)
Step 4: Verify each chapter has index (number), title (string), and summary (string)

description: POST /api/chapters returns 4xx when youtubeUrl is missing or invalid
steps: Step 1: POST to /api/chapters with empty body {}
Step 2: Verify HTTP status is 4xx
Step 3: POST to /api/chapters with invalid URL
Step 4: Verify HTTP status is 4xx with clear error message

description: POST /api/generate accepts selectedChapters array and only includes selected chapters in output
steps: Step 1: First call POST /api/chapters to get chapter list
Step 2: POST to /api/generate with selectedChapters: [0, 2] (select chapters 1 and 3)
Step 3: Verify SSE stream only contains content for chapters at indices 0 and 2
Step 4: Verify chapters at other indices are NOT in the output

description: Default mode (no custom rules): article preserves original subtitle wording — only chapter headings are added
steps: Step 1: POST to /api/generate with youtubeUrl, no rule field
Step 2: Verify the SSE stream output preserves the original subtitle text wording
Step 3: Verify chapter headings are inserted at topic boundaries
Step 4: Verify no AI rewriting or summarization occurs in the output

description: Custom rule mode (rules provided): AI generates article strictly following user requirements
steps: Step 1: POST to /api/generate with youtubeUrl and rule containing style=concise
Step 2: Verify the output is rewritten (not original subtitle wording)
Step 3: Verify the output follows the concise style requirement
Step 4: Compare output with and without rule — verify they are different

description: Chapter headings are rendered as h2 elements with natural line breaks, no border boxes
steps: Step 1: Generate an article with multiple chapters
Step 2: Verify each ## heading is rendered as an h2 element
Step 3: Verify chapters are separated by natural line breaks, not bordered boxes
Step 4: Verify no .chapter-block elements are present

description: Generate button shows disabled state during generation and loading text (生成中...)
steps: Step 1: Enter a valid YouTube URL and click Generate
Step 2: Verify Generate button is disabled during the request
Step 3: Verify button text changes to '生成中...'
Step 4: Wait for completion and verify button re-enables with text '开始生成'

description: Custom rules text field is shown in the input area, visible from page load
steps: Step 1: Open the page — verify input area has URL field, custom rules field, model selector, and Generate button
Step 2: Verify custom rules textarea is visible immediately without any additional step
Step 3: Verify rules field is optional

description: Result area shows streaming content during generation; article is displayed after completion
steps: Step 1: Enter a valid YouTube URL and click Generate
Step 2: Verify loading spinner and result area are shown
Step 3: Verify article content streams into the result area in real-time
Step 4: After completion, verify full article with chapter headings and 5W1H buttons is displayed

description: Default output language is Chinese; user can override via custom rules
steps: Step 1: Generate article without any rule — verify output is in Chinese
Step 2: Generate article with rule containing output language preference for English
Step 3: Verify the rule-specified language takes effect in the output
Step 4: Verify default Chinese resumes when no language rule is given

description: POST /api/chapters respects ?provider= query parameter for chapter detection
steps: Step 1: POST to /api/chapters?provider=deepseek with valid youtubeUrl
Step 2: Verify DeepSeek is used for chapter detection
Step 3: POST to /api/chapters without provider parameter
Step 4: Verify Gemini is used by default

description: Loading spinner is shown during article generation, hidden on completion or error
steps: Step 1: Enter a valid YouTube URL and click Generate
Step 2: Verify loading spinner appears immediately
Step 3: Verify loading spinner is hidden when streaming completes or on error

description: Model selector dropdown is shown and appends ?provider= to fetch URLs
steps: Step 1: Open the page
Step 2: Verify a model selector dropdown is visible with Gemini 2.5 Flash (default) and DeepSeek V4 Flash options
Step 3: Select DeepSeek and verify the dropdown value changes
Step 4: Verify the fetch URL for /api/chapters includes ?provider=deepseek when DeepSeek is selected

description: Default mode detect topic boundaries and merges same-topic content into chapters
steps: Step 1: POST to /api/generate with subtitle text containing multiple topics, no rule field
Step 2: Verify the output groups same-topic content under one ## chapter heading
Step 3: Verify topic transitions are marked with new ## chapter headings
Step 4: Verify content within a chapter is topically coherent

description: Default mode formats dialogue with speaker name prefixes (Name: content), one speaker per line
steps: Step 1: POST to /api/generate with subtitle text containing conversation between multiple speakers, no rule field
Step 2: Verify each speaker line is prefixed with their name (e.g., Jen: ..., Mark: ...)
Step 3: Verify each speaker turn is on its own line
Step 4: Verify original dialogue wording is preserved (no rewriting)

description: Default mode handles multiple participants — each person's dialogue formatted individually by topic
steps: Step 1: POST to /api/generate with subtitle text containing 3+ speakers across multiple topics, no rule field
Step 2: Verify each speaker's content is formatted with their name prefix
Step 3: Verify dialogue is organized by topic under ## chapter headings
Step 4: Verify all participants are represented in the output

description: Default mode preserves original wording — dialogue and narration are not rewritten or summarized
steps: Step 1: POST to /api/generate with subtitle text, no rule field
Step 2: Verify output text matches original subtitle wording verbatim
Step 3: Verify chapters are added but content is not paraphrased
Step 4: Verify no AI-generated commentary or analysis is inserted

description: Default mode dialogue format example is included in prompt to guide AI output format
steps: Step 1: Check prompts.ts buildArticlePrompt and buildArticleMessages
Step 2: Verify default mode includes dialogue format example with Jen/Mark/Sarah
Step 3: Verify example shows speaker prefix format and multi-line dialogue
Step 4: Verify example appears in both Gemini (plain text) and DeepSeek (chat messages) prompts

description: Default mode translates non-Chinese subtitles to Chinese; Chinese subtitles are kept as-is
steps: Step 1: POST to /api/generate with English subtitle text, no rule field
Step 2: Verify the output is in Chinese
Step 3: Verify the original meaning is preserved in translation
Step 4: POST to /api/generate with Chinese subtitle text, no rule field
Step 5: Verify Chinese output is preserved verbatim (no redundant translation)
